title: Trump Is Urged to Act on Iranian Site Feared Impervious to Airstrikes
summary: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

title: Oil Prices Fall Sharply After Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Open
summary: But analysts said it was not clear how quickly the oil industry in the Persian Gulf would be able to get back to normal.

title: Stocks Extend Rally as Tensions Ease Over War in Iran
summary: The S&P 500’s rise on Friday caps a striking three-week streak, powered by investors’ optimism about the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and strong corporate earnings.

title: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, Detained by ICE in Alabama, Is Released
summary: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, 85, who was arrested amid an inheritance dispute, has returned to France, its foreign affairs minister said. She came to America last year after reconnecting with and marrying a former G.I.

title: She Made Sure Her Baby Was Born an American. Then Federal Agents Separated Them.
summary: Diana Acosta Verde, who came into the United States illegally when she was six months pregnant, had to leave her baby at a hospital while she returned to a detention center.

title: Rumeysa Ozturk, Tufts Student Held in Immigration Detention, Returns to Turkey
summary: Rumeysa Ozturk, who was detained for weeks by the Trump administration after co-writing a pro-Palestinian opinion essay, has graduated and returned home.

title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
summary: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.

title: Lutnick Says Canada Trade Deal Needs to Be Reworked Ahead of Talks
summary: Howard Lutnick, President Trump’s commerce secretary, derided Canada’s trade strategy and said a North American deal needed to be reworked.

title: Aging in a Brightly Lit, Big City: Jay McInerney, With a New Novel
summary: In 1984, Jay McInerney was a famous, young, hedonistic novelist. Now 71, he is wistful as he wraps up his tetralogy about a couple whose city, and marriage, are tested by the pandemic.

title: Hundreds of Fake Pro-Trump Avatars Emerge on Social Media
summary: The artificial-intelligence-generated fake influencers have surged on TikTok, Instagram, Facebook and YouTube in an apparent bid to hook conservative voters.

title: Obama Urges Virginians to Vote ‘Yes’ on Redistricting Referendum
summary: Democrats may win a vote to give their party more House seats, but they are growing anxious — in part because of dueling TV ads that might confuse voters about where the former president stands.

title: Texas Democrat James Talarico Is Out-Raising His G.O.P. Opponents
summary: James Talarico, the Democratic nominee, has been amassing campaign cash as Senator John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton still battle each other.

title: Lena Dunham Made Millennial Culture. Then She Was Undone by It.
summary: The era of “Girls” is long gone, but its creator still has much to teach us.

title: In ‘Famesick,’ Lena Dunham Diagnoses Celebrity, Illness and Herself
summary: This unusually unfiltered memoir takes us to the hospital, to therapy and to the sometimes hostile set of “Girls.”

title: Supreme Court Sides With Oil Companies in Louisiana Coastal Lawsuits
summary: The companies had asked the justices to clear the way to move environmental lawsuits out of state courts, to friendlier federal venues.

title: Through A.I. Glasses-Powered Translation, Korea’s Theaters Hope for a K-Pop Moment
summary: Producers and the cultural authorities hope that technology can overcome a language barrier and take the country’s shows to the world.

title: How Do You Measure an A.I. Boom?
summary: A chart created by METR, a nonprofit A.I. organization, has become an industrywide obsession as it measures the rapid development of big A.I. systems.

title: Trump Tries to Stop Drug Smuggling From South America. Drug Smugglers Invent New Tactics.
summary: Authorities are using drones, troops and A.I. to crack down on cocaine smuggling. But gangs are finding new ways to move record amounts of drugs across the world.

title: How the D4vd Case and Rumors Captivated the Public Before the Singer’s Arrest
summary: A teenage girl went missing. The police found her remains in a musician’s car. Then the Los Angeles media machine got to work.

title: Who Are D4vd and Celeste Rivas Hernandez?
summary: The musician is known for creating the anthem for Fortnite. He was touring for his major-label debut when Ms. Rivas Hernandez’s remains were found in his car, officials said.

title: A Century Later, the Flatiron’s Revolving Door Is Restored
summary: The revolving door’s inventor built this one over 100 years ago. It was reinstalled this week.

title: A Paris Court Just Rewrote the Rules of Corporate Morality
summary: The profit motive was on trial. The verdict was scathing.

title: Orban Lost. But Populism Is Very Much Alive.
summary: Critics of populists need to do more than thrive off the missteps of their opponents.

title: A Huge Arch
summary: President Trump wants to build one, but he’s getting pushback.

title: Stephen Colbert Delights in Trump’s ‘Ongoing Papal Feud’
summary: The host of “The Late Show” recapped what he called “yet another day when the entire world is on edge over President Trump’s senseless and elective war — with the pope.”

title: NJ Transit Confirms It Will Charge $150 for Train Tickets During the World Cup
summary: Tens of thousands of people are expected to attend matches at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey this summer, but most will not be allowed to drive there.

title: Midwest Forecast to See Strong Storms on Friday
summary: The Midwest has faced day after day of weather warnings this week. More are expected on Friday.

title: Help for Medicare Advantage Patients Who Lose Doctors Is Shelved, for Now
summary: Nationwide, hospitals and other providers are leaving private Medicare Advantage plans, putting thousands of seniors at risk of higher costs and of losing trusted doctors.
